summary: First testing run for KDIPL B2B landing page MVP. Backend pytest suite (24 tests) all pass — covers lead CRUD for all 3 lead types, validation (422 for missing/invalid fields), admin login (correct & wrong password), token verify, list filters (type/status/q), stats, status update, delete, CSV export, MongoDB _id excluded, and graceful Resend skip when key empty. Frontend Playwright tests all pass — landing page renders all sections, tab switching shows conditional fields (quantity/territory/experience/expected_volume), form submission posts to /api/leads with success toast and form reset, WhatsApp FAB has correct wa.me/919311342988 href with target=_blank. Admin panel: wrong password shows error toast, correct login lands on dashboard, stats grid renders, leads table populates, type filter tabs filter rows, search filters by name, View dialog opens with details, inline status update select works (Status updated to qualified), CSV export downloads kdipl-leads-YYYY-MM-DD.csv, logout returns to login page. No console errors aside from the expected 401 from intentional wrong-password test.
critical_code_review_comments: ADMIN_TOKEN_SECRET in .env is 'change-me-in-prod-kdipl-2025' — strong enough for MVP but consider rotating before production launch.

send_lead_email is fire-and-forget via asyncio.create_task — if the task raises before the try/except inside, exception would be unhandled. Currently safe because the body is wrapped, but worth noting.
POST /api/leads returns 200 (FastAPI default) instead of 201 Created — minor REST convention deviation, not blocking.
CSV export limit hardcoded to 5000 — fine for MVP scale, document if expected to grow.

Lead model has no rate-limiting / spam protection on the public POST /api/leads endpoint. Consider adding hCaptcha/honeypot or Cloudflare rate limit before launch to avoid form spam (especially with email notifications enabled later).
Admin token stored in localStorage — acceptable for MVP password-only admin; XSS would expose it. Note for future hardening (httpOnly cookie + CSRF).
test_credentials: Admin password: kdipl@admin2025 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md and /app/backend/.env). No LLM/Resend keys; Resend correctly skips when RESEND_API_KEY empty.
seed_data_creation: Test leads created during pytest run are cleaned up by test_zz_cleanup; UI test lead deleted manually via admin API after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_kdipl_api.py is the regression suite — run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_kdipl_api.py -v. It auto-cleans created leads. Admin panel uses data-testid attributes throughout (admin-login-page, admin-password-input, admin-login-btn, admin-dashboard, stats-grid, leads-table, lead-row-{id}, status-select-{id}, actions-{id}, filter-type-{all|sample|quote|distributor}, filter-status, search-input, refresh-btn, export-csv-btn, admin-logout-btn, lead-detail-dialog). Landing page uses contact-section, lead-form-tabs, tab-{sample|quote|distributor}, input-{name|email|phone|company|country|city|product|quantity|territory|experience|volume|message}, submit-lead-btn, landing-page. Stats card uses conditional render so wait for [data-testid=stats-grid] after dashboard loads (1-2s).
rca of the issue: No bugs found — all 24 backend tests + full frontend flow passed on first run. Initial 'stats-grid not visible' observation was a timing race in test (stats are conditionally rendered after async load) and resolved with proper wait_for_selector.
